```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 

# Input and Output Directories
metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
plots_dir    <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# Create the Plots directory if it doesn't exist
dir.create(plots_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD REGIONAL MASTER DATA
# =====================================================================
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))

cat("Loading Master Regional Data...\n")
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
all_preds <- master_regional_data$All_Predictions

# =====================================================================
# 3. DEFINE REGIONAL Q-Q PLOTTING FUNCTION (Calib = Black, Valid by Scale)
# =====================================================================
plot_regional_qq <- function(df, stat_name, y_label) {
  
  # 1. Filter predictions for the specific statistic
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Actual)) %>% 
    mutate(
      Data_Type = ifelse(Scale_k >= 24, "Calibration data", "Validation data"),
      # Convert Scale_k to a factor and sort it so the legend is in numeric order
      Scale_k_factor = factor(Scale_k, levels = sort(unique(Scale_k)))
    )
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Pivot data to long format so we can facet by Model
  model_cols <- setdiff(names(df_stat), c("Scale_k", "Scale_k_factor", "Statistic", "Actual", "Data_Type", "Station_ID"))
  
  df_long <- df_stat %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(model_cols), names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Predicted))
  
  if(nrow(df_long) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 3. Find global min/max so the X and Y axes perfectly match (true 1:1 plot)
  min_val <- min(c(df_long$Actual, df_long$Predicted), na.rm = TRUE)
  max_val <- max(c(df_long$Actual, df_long$Predicted), na.rm = TRUE)
  buffer <- (max_val - min_val) * 0.05
  if(buffer == 0) buffer <- 0.05
  
  # 4. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, shape = Data_Type)) +
    # The 1:1 perfect agreement line
    geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.8) +
    
    # Layer 1: Calibration Points (Strictly Black)
    geom_point(data = subset(df_long, Data_Type == "Calibration data"), 
               color = "black", alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
    
    # Layer 2: Validation Points (Colored by Scale k)
    geom_point(data = subset(df_long, Data_Type == "Validation data"), 
               aes(color = Scale_k_factor), alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
    
    # Create separate panels for each model 
    facet_wrap(~ Model) + 
    
    # Formatting
    scale_color_viridis_d(name = "Valid. Scale (k)", option = "turbo") + 
    scale_shape_manual(name = "Data Type", values = c("Calibration data" = 16, "Validation data" = 17)) +
    
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(min_val - buffer, max_val + buffer)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(min_val - buffer, max_val + buffer)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + 
    
    labs(
      title = bquote("Regional Empirical vs Predicted:" ~ .(y_label[[1]])),
      x = bquote("Empirical (Actual)" ~ .(y_label[[1]])),
      y = bquote("Predicted" ~ .(y_label[[1]]))
    )  +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(linetype = "dotted", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "whitesmoke"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right", 
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    # Ensure point colors override alpha transparency in the legend
    guides(
      color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1, size = 3), order = 1),
      # Set legend colors to black for Calib and gray for Valid so the shapes represent the map accurately
      shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = c("black", "gray50"), alpha = 1, size = 3), order = 2)
    )
  
  return(p)
}
# =====================================================================
# 4. GENERATE AND EXPORT THE PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Q-Q plots...\n")

qq_p_zero <- plot_regional_qq(all_preds, "p_zero", expression(p[0]^{(k)}))
qq_t2     <- plot_regional_qq(all_preds, "t2_pos", expression(t[2]^{(k)}))
qq_t3     <- plot_regional_qq(all_preds, "t3_pos", expression(t[3]^{(k)}))
qq_t4     <- plot_regional_qq(all_preds, "t4_pos", expression(t[4]^{(k)}))

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork
qq_list <- list(qq_p_zero, qq_t2, qq_t3, qq_t4)
qq_list <- qq_list[!sapply(qq_list, is.null)] # Remove empty plots safely

if(length(qq_list) > 0) {
  combined_qq_plots <- wrap_plots(qq_list, ncol = 2) + 
    plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  # Save the large validation grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_QQ_Validation_Plots.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_qq_plots, width = 14, height = 12, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined plots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your All_Predictions data.\n")
}
```



```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales) # Needed to extract the turbo palette colors

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES (Assuming master data is already loaded)
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# (If you need to reload the data, uncomment the lines below)
# metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
# master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
# master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
# all_preds <- master_regional_data$All_Predictions

# =====================================================================
# 2. DEFINE MSE BARPLOT FUNCTION
# =====================================================================
plot_regional_mse_bars <- function(df, stat_name, y_label) {
  
  # 1. Filter for the specific statistic
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Actual))
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Pivot to long format to access all Models
  model_cols <- setdiff(names(df_stat), c("Scale_k", "Statistic", "Actual", "Station_ID", "Data_Type"))
  
  # 3. Calculate Regional MSE per Scale and Model
  df_mse <- df_stat %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(model_cols), names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Predicted)) %>%
    group_by(Scale_k, Model) %>%
    summarize(MSE = mean((Actual - Predicted)^2, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(
      Data_Type = ifelse(Scale_k >= 24, "Calib. data", "Valid. data"),
      Scale_k_factor = factor(Scale_k, levels = sort(unique(Scale_k)))
    )
  
  if(nrow(df_mse) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 4. Create Custom Color Palette
  # Validation (< 24) gets vibrant colors; Calibration (>= 24) gets grey
  unique_scales <- sort(unique(df_mse$Scale_k))
  valid_scales <- unique_scales[unique_scales < 24]
  calib_scales <- unique_scales[unique_scales >= 24]
  
  # Extract hex codes from the "turbo" palette for validation scales
  valid_colors <- scales::viridis_pal(option = "turbo")(length(valid_scales))
  calib_colors <- rep("grey60", length(calib_scales)) # uniform grey for training
  
  # Map scales to colors
  custom_colors <- setNames(c(valid_colors, calib_colors), 
                            c(as.character(valid_scales), as.character(calib_scales)))
  
  # 5. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_mse, aes(x = Model, y = MSE, fill = Scale_k_factor)) +
    # Group bars by Model, dodge by Scale_k
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.85), width = 0.8, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
    
    # Apply custom fill colors
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Scale k (hours)", values = custom_colors) +
    
    labs(
      title = bquote("Regional MSE by Model:" ~ .(y_label[[1]])),
      x = "Theoretical Model",
      y = "Mean Squared Error (MSE)"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), # Remove vertical grid lines for cleaner bars
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "whitesmoke"),
      legend.position = "right",
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, vjust = 1, hjust = 1) # Slight tilt for model names
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE AND EXPORT PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Regional MSE Barplots...\n")

bar_p_zero <- plot_regional_mse_bars(all_preds, "p_zero", expression(p[0]^{(k)}))
bar_t2     <- plot_regional_mse_bars(all_preds, "t2_pos", expression(t[2]^{(k)}))
bar_t3     <- plot_regional_mse_bars(all_preds, "t3_pos", expression(t[3]^{(k)}))
bar_t4     <- plot_regional_mse_bars(all_preds, "t4_pos", expression(t[4]^{(k)}))

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork
bar_list <- list(bar_p_zero, bar_t2, bar_t3, bar_t4)
bar_list <- bar_list[!sapply(bar_list, is.null)] # Remove empty plots safely

if(length(bar_list) > 0) {
  combined_bar_plots <- wrap_plots(bar_list, ncol = 2) + 
    plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
    theme(legend.position = "right") # Collect the legend on the right
  
  # Save the large validation grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_MSE_Barplots.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_bar_plots, width = 14, height = 10, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined MSE bar plots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your All_Predictions data.\n")
}
```



```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales) 

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# =====================================================================
# 2. DEFINE SQUARED ERROR BOXPLOT FUNCTION
# =====================================================================
plot_regional_se_boxplots <- function(df, stat_name, y_label) {
  
  # 1. Filter for the specific statistic
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Actual))
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Pivot to long format to access all Models
  model_cols <- setdiff(names(df_stat), c("Scale_k", "Statistic", "Actual", "Station_ID", "Data_Type"))
  
  # 3. Calculate Squared Error per Station, Scale, and Model
  # We do NOT summarize here, so we keep the distribution for the boxplot
  df_se <- df_stat %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(model_cols), names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Predicted)) %>%
    mutate(
      Squared_Error = (Actual - Predicted)^2,
      Data_Type = ifelse(Scale_k >= 24, "Calib. data", "Valid. data"),
      Scale_k_factor = factor(Scale_k, levels = sort(unique(Scale_k)))
    )
  
  if(nrow(df_se) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 4. Create Custom Color Palette
  # Validation (< 24) gets vibrant colors; Calibration (>= 24) gets grey
  unique_scales <- sort(unique(df_se$Scale_k))
  valid_scales <- unique_scales[unique_scales < 24]
  calib_scales <- unique_scales[unique_scales >= 24]
  
  valid_colors <- scales::viridis_pal(option = "turbo")(length(valid_scales))
  calib_colors <- rep("grey60", length(calib_scales)) 
  
  # Map scales to colors
  custom_colors <- setNames(c(valid_colors, calib_colors), 
                            c(as.character(valid_scales), as.character(calib_scales)))
  
  # 5. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_se, aes(x = Model, y = Squared_Error, fill = Scale_k_factor)) +
    # Group boxplots by Model, dodge by Scale_k
    geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.85), width = 0.7, 
                 outlier.size = 0.5, outlier.alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.3) +
    
    # Apply custom fill colors
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Scale k (hours)", values = custom_colors) +
    
    labs(
      title = bquote("Regional SE Dist.:" ~ .(y_label[[1]])),
      x = "Theoretical Model",
      y = "Squared Error (SE)"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), 
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "whitesmoke"),
      legend.position = "right",
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, vjust = 1, hjust = 1) 
    )
  
  # NOTE: Squared Errors often have massive outliers. 
  # If your boxes are getting squashed flat, uncomment the next line to use a logarithmic scale:
  # p <- p + scale_y_log10() + ylab("Squared Error (Log10 Scale)")
  
  return(p)
}

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE AND EXPORT PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Regional SE Boxplots...\n")

box_p_zero <- plot_regional_se_boxplots(all_preds, "p_zero", expression(p[0]^{(k)}))
box_t2     <- plot_regional_se_boxplots(all_preds, "t2_pos", expression(t[2]^{(k)}))
box_t3     <- plot_regional_se_boxplots(all_preds, "t3_pos", expression(t[3]^{(k)}))
box_t4     <- plot_regional_se_boxplots(all_preds, "t4_pos", expression(t[4]^{(k)}))

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork
box_list <- list(box_p_zero, box_t2, box_t3, box_t4)
box_list <- box_list[!sapply(box_list, is.null)] 

if(length(box_list) > 0) {
  combined_box_plots <- wrap_plots(box_list, ncol = 2) + 
    plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
    theme(legend.position = "right") 
  
  # Save the large validation grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_SE_Boxplots.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_box_plots, width = 14, height = 10, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined SE boxplots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your All_Predictions data.\n")
}
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# (If you need to reload the master data, uncomment the lines below)
# metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
# master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
# master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)

# Extract the evaluated models dataframe (contains mse_validation)
eval_models <- master_regional_data$Final_Evaluated_Models

# =====================================================================
# 2. DEFINE ECDF PLOTTING FUNCTION
# =====================================================================
plot_ecdf_mse <- function(df, stat_name, title_expr) {
  
  # 1. Filter for the specific statistic and remove NA validation MSEs
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(mse_validation))
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Ensure models are factors so the legend order and mapping are consistent
  expected_models <- c("Weibull_1p", "Weibull_2p", "PowerLaw_1p", "PowerLaw_2p")
  df_stat$Model <- factor(df_stat$Model, levels = expected_models)
  
  
  
  # 4. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_stat, aes(x = mse_validation, color = Model, linetype = Model)) +
    # Use step function for ECDF
    stat_ecdf(geom = "step", linewidth = 1) +
    
    # Map colors to match the uploaded image (Wei = Blue, Lom/PowerLaw = Red)
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Weibull_1p" = "blue", "Weibull_2p" = "blue",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "red", "PowerLaw_2p" = "red"),
      labels = c("Weibull_1p" = "Wei-1p", "Weibull_2p" = "Wei-2p",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "Lom-1p", "PowerLaw_2p" = "Lom-2p")
    ) +
    
    # Map linetypes to match the uploaded image (1p = Solid, 2p = Dashed)
    scale_linetype_manual(
      values = c("Weibull_1p" = "solid", "Weibull_2p" = "dashed",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "solid", "PowerLaw_2p" = "dashed"),
      labels = c("Weibull_1p" = "Wei-1p", "Weibull_2p" = "Wei-2p",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "Lom-1p", "PowerLaw_2p" = "Lom-2p")
    ) +
    
    
    labs(
      title = bquote("Statistic:" ~ .(title_expr[[1]])),
      x = expression(epsilon ~ "(MSE)"),
      y = expression("Prob(" * epsilon <= x * ")")
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      
      # Boxed legend styling similar to the image
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.position.inside = c(0.8, 0.2), # Adjust coordinates if legend overlaps curves
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.background = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE AND EXPORT PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Regional ECDF Plots...\n")

ecdf_p_zero <- plot_ecdf_mse(eval_models, "p_zero", expression(p[0]))
ecdf_t2     <- plot_ecdf_mse(eval_models, "t2_pos", expression(tau[2]^"+"))
ecdf_t3     <- plot_ecdf_mse(eval_models, "t3_pos", expression(tau[3]^"+"))
ecdf_t4     <- plot_ecdf_mse(eval_models, "t4_pos", expression(tau[4]^"+"))

# Remove the legend from all plots except the first one for a cleaner grid
if (!is.null(ecdf_t2)) ecdf_t2 <- ecdf_t2 + theme(legend.position = "none")
if (!is.null(ecdf_t3)) ecdf_t3 <- ecdf_t3 + theme(legend.position = "none")
if (!is.null(ecdf_t4)) ecdf_t4 <- ecdf_t4 + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork (1 row, 4 columns)
ecdf_list <- list(ecdf_p_zero, ecdf_t2, ecdf_t3, ecdf_t4)
ecdf_list <- ecdf_list[!sapply(ecdf_list, is.null)] 

if(length(ecdf_list) > 0) {
  combined_ecdf_plots <- wrap_plots(ecdf_list, nrow = 1) 
  
  # Save the grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_ECDF_MSE.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_ecdf_plots, width = 16, height = 4.5, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined ECDF plots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your Final_Evaluated_Models data.\n")
}
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# (If you need to reload the master data, uncomment the lines below)
# metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
# master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
# master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)

# Extract all predictions to calculate errors per scale dynamically
all_preds <- master_regional_data$All_Predictions

# =====================================================================
# 2. DEFINE APE ECDF PLOTTING FUNCTION
# =====================================================================
plot_ecdf_ape <- function(df, stat_name, title_expr) {
  
  # 1. Filter for specific statistic and VALIDATION scales only (< 24)
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name, Scale_k < 24) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Actual))
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Pivot to long format to access all Models
  model_cols <- intersect(names(df_stat), c("Weibull_1p", "Weibull_2p", "PowerLaw_1p", "PowerLaw_2p"))
  
  df_long <- df_stat %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(model_cols), names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Predicted)) %>%
    
    # 3. Calculate Absolute Percentage Error (APE) based on formula
    mutate(APE = 100 * abs(Predicted - Actual) / Actual) %>%
    
    # Remove Inf/NaNs just in case an Actual value was exactly 0
    filter(is.finite(APE))
  
  if(nrow(df_long) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 4. Ensure models are factors so the legend order is consistent
  expected_models <- c("Weibull_1p", "Weibull_2p", "PowerLaw_1p", "PowerLaw_2p")
  df_long$Model <- factor(df_long$Model, levels = expected_models)
  

  
  # 6. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = APE, color = Model, linetype = Model)) +
    # Step function for ECDF
    stat_ecdf(geom = "step", linewidth = 1) +
    
    # Vertical reference line at x = 5 (matching your image)
    geom_vline(xintercept = 5, linetype = "solid", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    
    # Map colors to match the uploaded image (Wei = Blue, Lom/PowerLaw = Red)
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Weibull_1p" = "blue", "Weibull_2p" = "blue",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "red", "PowerLaw_2p" = "red"),
      labels = c("Weibull_1p" = "Wei-1p", "Weibull_2p" = "Wei-2p",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "Lom-1p", "PowerLaw_2p" = "Lom-2p")
    ) +
    
    # Map linetypes to match the uploaded image (1p = Solid, 2p = Dashed)
    scale_linetype_manual(
      values = c("Weibull_1p" = "solid", "Weibull_2p" = "dashed",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "solid", "PowerLaw_2p" = "dashed"),
      labels = c("Weibull_1p" = "Wei-1p", "Weibull_2p" = "Wei-2p",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "Lom-1p", "PowerLaw_2p" = "Lom-2p")
    ) +
    

    labs(
      title = bquote("Statistic:" ~ .(title_expr[[1]])),
      x = expression(epsilon ~ "[%]"),
      y = expression("Prob(" * epsilon <= x * ")")
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      
      # Boxed legend styling
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.position.inside = c(0.75, 0.25), 
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.background = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE AND EXPORT PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Regional APE ECDF Plots...\n")

ecdf_ape_p_zero <- plot_ecdf_ape(all_preds, "p_zero", expression(p[0]))
ecdf_ape_t2     <- plot_ecdf_ape(all_preds, "t2_pos", expression(tau[2]^"+"))
ecdf_ape_t3     <- plot_ecdf_ape(all_preds, "t3_pos", expression(tau[3]^"+"))
ecdf_ape_t4     <- plot_ecdf_ape(all_preds, "t4_pos", expression(tau[4]^"+"))

# Remove the legend from all plots except the first one for a cleaner grid
if (!is.null(ecdf_ape_t2)) ecdf_ape_t2 <- ecdf_ape_t2 + theme(legend.position = "none")
if (!is.null(ecdf_ape_t3)) ecdf_ape_t3 <- ecdf_ape_t3 + theme(legend.position = "none")
if (!is.null(ecdf_ape_t4)) ecdf_ape_t4 <- ecdf_ape_t4 + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork (1 row, 4 columns)
ecdf_ape_list <- list(ecdf_ape_p_zero, ecdf_ape_t2, ecdf_ape_t3, ecdf_ape_t4)
ecdf_ape_list <- ecdf_ape_list[!sapply(ecdf_ape_list, is.null)] 

if(length(ecdf_ape_list) > 0) {
  combined_ecdf_ape_plots <- wrap_plots(ecdf_ape_list, nrow = 1) 
  
  # Save the grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_ECDF_APE.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_ecdf_ape_plots, width = 16, height = 4.5, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined APE ECDF plots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your All_Predictions data.\n")
}
```
